item.title: Nvidia Invests $2 Billion in Marvell, Announces Partnership
item.description: Nvidia Corp. said it has invested $2 billion in Marvell Technology Inc. and that the two chipmakers plan to collaborate on silicon photonics technology.

item.title: Huawei Growth Stalls After Apple Comeback Offsets China AI Push
item.description: Huawei Technologies Co.’s growth decelerated sharply after Apple Inc.’s iPhone 17 made headway in China and domestic challengers in AI chipmaking emerged.

item.title: Tether Cuts Top Gold Traders Months After Hiring Them From HSBC
item.description: Tether Holdings SA has cut two senior precious metals traders who had joined from HSBC Holdings Plc just months ago as part of the stablecoin issuer’s plan to build “the best trading floor for gold in the world.”

item.title: Private Credit Poses ‘Chain Reaction’ Risk to US, DZ Bank Says
item.description: Germany’s second-largest lender warned that private credit is now so big, it poses a major risk to the US economy.

item.title: CoreWeave Raises $8.5 Billion GPU Loan Backed by Meta Deal
item.description: CoreWeave Inc. has raised $8.5 billion from a group of banks and investors to help finance an expansion of its cloud computing capacity in what the company says is the largest chip-backed debt deal of its kind.

item.title: Google Partner Tenex Raises $250 Million for AI Security Services
item.description: The funding round values the cybersecurity startup at more than $1 billion.

item.title: A Tribal Group in California Vies for a Piece of the AI Boom
item.description: In California’s Sacramento River Valley, a lush agricultural region known for producing rice and alfalfa, one tribal group is taking steps to claim a piece of the global AI boom.

item.title: Pepkor Eyes 2027 for the Rollout of a New South African Bank
item.description: Pepkor Holdings Ltd. hired Merwe Scholtz to head its new banking initiative as Africa’s largest seller of clothing and mobile phones prepares to operate an independent lender from next year.

item.title: US Stock Futures Jump as Trump Signals Desire to End Iran War
item.description: Contracts tied to US stocks advanced early Tuesday as President Donald Trump indicated he’s willing to end America’s military campaign against Iran even if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed.

item.title: Wild Charts Show Pain Points in S&P 500’s Worst Month Since 2022
item.description: Tech megacaps entered a correction, oil prices broke out, big-money funds retreated and small-lot investors showed waning conviction in buying the dip. While the war in Iran that triggered all that didn’t end a three-year bull run in US equities, it is shaking it to its core.

item.title: Crude Oil Prices Don’t Reflect the Global Supply Crisis
item.description: While Brent and WTI hover around the low $100s, spiking fuel costs paint a clearer picture of the escalating disruption.

item.title: Germany’s May Power Prices Surge to Four Times French Levels
item.description: German power prices for May are now trading at roughly four times the level in France, a record multiple as the disruption to natural gas supplies from the Iran war amplifies longstanding differences between the two markets.

item.title: ‘Uninvestable’ Super Micro Sends Investors Racing to the Exits
item.description: On paper, Super Micro Computer Inc. is the type of company that Wall Street can’t get enough of, with soaring sales, an enviable list of partners like Nvidia Corp. and its placement at the center of the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Russian Fuel Cargo Shows Ghana’s Resilience to War Supply Shock
item.description: Ghana is better insulated from fuel-supply disruptions linked to the Iran conflict than many sub-Saharan African peers thanks to its range of sources, including shipments from Russia.

item.title: War’s First Inflation Hit Arrives in the Euro Area
item.description: The first hard data on the initial fallout for consumers in the euro zone shows the biggest inflation jump since 2022

item.title: Sony’s $900 PS5 Pro Is the Latest Unprecedented Thing of 2026
item.description: The trend toward pricier electronics is pushing up the price of everything

item.title: Microsoft Business Software Ecosystem Faces UK Competition Probe
item.description: Microsoft Corp.’s business software ecosystem faces a UK antitrust probe starting in May to decide whether licensing practices for products such as Word, Excel and AI app Copilot need tighter regulation.

item.title: From Australia to California, Momentum Builds to Curb Kids’ Access to Social Media
item.description: The push to protect children from social media’s harms is gaining momentum globally and beginning to reach Big Tech’s home turf in the US.

item.title: Delta Deal Gives Amazon Big Win in Airline Battle Against SpaceX
item.description: Delta Air Lines Inc. will use Amazon.com Inc.’s satellites to deliver in-flight Wi-Fi service, a big win for the e-commerce giant’s space business as it vies with Elon Musk’s Starlink for aviation customers.

item.title: US Sees ‘Limited Role’ for WTO After Ministerial Meeting Yields Little
item.description: Top US trade official expresses disappointment at WTO’s failure to reach agreement on digital trade rules

item.title: Cross River Raises $50 Million to Expand Further Into AI, Crypto
item.description: Cross River Bank has tapped existing investors for $50 million in fresh capital, as the fintech-focused lender doubles down on artificial intelligence and cryptocurrency, according to a statement reviewed by Bloomberg News.

item.title: Canadian Tech Stocks Set For Worst Quarter Since 2022 on AI, War
item.description: Canada’s tech stocks are headed for their worst quarter in nearly four years as the software-heavy sector was hit by fears about artificial intelligence-related disruption and the Middle East conflict sent capital toward energy names.

item.title: Ethiopia Keeps Key Rate Flat Citing Need to Contain Inflation
item.description: Ethiopia’s central bank retained its benchmark interest rate at 15%, saying a tight monetary policy stance remains necessary to keep inflation below 10%.

item.title: Tech Bulls Ready to Buy as Rout Crushes Valuations
item.description: Nasdaq 100 valuation relative to S&P 500 is lowest since 2018.

item.title: Zhipu’s Losses Climb 60% After Chinese AI Rivalry Worsens
item.description: Zhipu reported a much faster-than-expected 60% surge in net losses for 2025, underscoring how China’s AI upstarts continue to spend aggressively on developing artificial intelligence to keep pace with rivals.

item.title: ECB’s Vujcic Says Rise in Inflation Expectations Isn’t Surprise
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Boris Vujcic said the increase in inflation expectations since the Iran war broke out isn’t a surprise.

item.title: Pakistan Weighs Hormuz Options After Iran Clears 20 Ships
item.description: Pakistan is considering options that include allowing other ships to ferry vital cargoes under its flag, after Iran said it would allow 20 of the nation’s vessels through the Strait of Hormuz — more than it currently has in the area.

item.title: Whoop Raises $575 Million at a $10 Billion Valuation on Its Way to an IPO
item.description: Whoop Inc., the maker of popular screenless fitness bands, is now valued at $10.1 billion after raising an additional $575 million, a new milestone on its way to an initial public offering.

item.title: Ramaphosa Courts Investment as South Africa Faces Iran War Headwinds
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa pitched South Africa as a reform-driven investment haven at a time of increasing global uncertainty, as he set a target off attracting 2 trillion rand ($117 billion) of capital to the country over the next five years.

item.title: Credit Data Firm 9fin Is Valued at $1.3 Billion in Funding Round
item.description: 9fin Ltd., the debt intelligence provider founded by two former investment banking analysts, has raised fresh funds at a $1.3 billion valuation as part of a push to capture a bigger share of the hotly contested market for credit research.
